title: Full-Time Content Creator (Nightlife, Events & Comedy) – Virtual Assistant
description:
Type: Full Time | Hours: 38 per week | Salary: $1,200 AUD/month | Paid: Weekly via Wise



?? READ THIS FIRST

If your portfolio is corporate explainers, fitness content, real estate walkthroughs, or talking-head videos — this is not the role for you.

We need someone who lives and breathes nightlife content, comedy-driven ads, and fast-turnaround event promotion. If that's not your lane, save us both the time.



About the Role

We are looking for a full-time Content Creator Virtual Assistant to own the entire content output for a single nightclub venue in Australia.

This is not a "cut clips and add subtitles" role. This is a full content creation role where you own the content engine for one venue.

You will be responsible for producing the complete content pipeline — paid ad creatives, organic social posts, short-form video (Reels, TikTok, Shorts), stories, event hype sequences, post-event recaps, UGC curation, and comedy/personality content that builds the venue's brand between events.

The venue runs on a weekly cycle. Every week has events. Every week needs a content arc — from teaser to hype to event night to recap. You need to understand that rhythm and own it without being told what to post every day.

This is a remote role based in the Philippines. You will not be on-site. Raw footage, photos, and assets will be provided to you by the venue team. However, you are expected to be proactive — you tell the venue what footage you need, what angles to capture, what 
nts to film. You direct the content pipeline. You don't just wait for files to land in your inbox.



This Role Is a Content Engine — Not Just Editing

This is not a general VA role.

This is not a strategy role.

This is not a "wait for briefs and execute" role.

The priority is:



Owning the weekly content cadence for the venue — you know what needs to go out and when

Building multi-day hype sequences before every event (teasers, DJ reveals, countdowns, last-call urgency posts)

Creating scroll-stopping paid ad content (video and static) designed to sell tickets

Producing high-energy organic social posts that drive engagement and build the venue's personality

Editing raw event footage into polished, fast-paced content — Reels, TikToks, Shorts, Stories

Creating post-event recaps, highlight reels, and UGC compilations that keep the venue top of mind

Producing comedy-driven and entertainment-style content that builds the brand between events

Proactively sending shot lists, content briefs, and footage requests to the venue team — you direct what gets captured on the ground

Sourcing and curating user-generated content from patrons (tagged stories, crowd footage, phone videos) and turning it into polished posts and ads



You must understand what makes people stop scrolling, laugh, share, and buy tickets.



Key Responsibilities

Primary Responsibilities (Core Focus)



Owning the weekly content calendar and storytelling arc for the venue

Sending content briefs, shot lists, and footage requests to the venue team before events — you tell them what you need captured

Editing short-form video content for Instagram Reels, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts from raw footage provided by the venue

Producing paid ad creatives (video and static) designed to convert and sell tickets

Creating organic social media posts that drive engagement

Building multi-day event hype sequences — teasers, DJ lineup reveals, countdown content, last-call urgency posts

Producing post-event recap content — highlight reels, UGC compilations, crowd reaction clips, best-of edits

Sourcing, curating, and repurposing user-generated content (patron videos, tagged stories, crowd footage)

Creating comedy, personality, and behind-the-scenes content that builds the venue's brand between events

Adding text overlays, subtitles, effects, transitions, and trending audio

Adapting one concept across multiple formats (Feed, Story, Square, Vertical, 9:16)

Delivering content on tight turnarounds — 6 to 24 hours when events demand it



Secondary Responsibilities (Support Tasks)



Organising content files in Google Drive

Tracking deliverables and deadlines in ClickUp

Following creative briefs and campaign deadlines from the marketing team

Preparing content exports correctly for each platform

Scheduling-ready content handoffs



(No paid ads management. No media buying. No marketing strategy.)



Required Experience (Non-Negotiable)

To be considered, you must have:



Experience creating content for nightclubs, DJs, festivals, live events, bars, comedy nights, or entertainment venues

Strong short-form video editing skills — not just trimming clips, but concept-to-finished-piece from raw footage

Experience producing paid ad creatives (video and/or static) that are designed to sell tickets or drive event attendance

Experience creating organic social content that drives real engagement

Understanding of weekly content cadence — you know how to tell a story across a week, building from teaser to hype to recap

Understanding of what makes comedy, entertainment, and nightlife content perform on social

Ability to work fast — this is an events business, not a "we'll get to it next week" environment

The ability to be proactive — you don't wait for someone to tell you what to make. You identify what's needed, request the right footage, and deliver

Evidence of content you've created that has gone viral or achieved significant reach (views, shares, engagement well above the norm for the account). Include specific examples with metrics if possible.

A portfolio that clearly demonstrates nightlife, events, comedy, or entertainment content



If your experience is mainly corporate, ecommerce product ads, fitness, real estate, or minimalist branding — this role is not suitable.



Nice to Have (Not Required)



Experience with ticket sale campaigns or event countdown sequences

Understanding of
/Instagram ad formats and specs

Experience creating UGC-style or native-feeling ad content

Motion graphics or animation skills

Experience working with Australian or Western nightlife brands

Basic graphic design ability (Canva or similar) for event posters, story graphics, and static ad creatives





Content Style We're Looking For



Fast-paced and high-energy

Bold text, punchy captions

Dark themes, neon, club lighting

Comedy timing and entertainment hooks

Native to the platform — not repurposed corporate content

Content that tells a story across the week, not random one-off posts

Content that feels like it belongs on a nightclub's page, not a brand agency's portfolio





What Success Looks Like in This Role



You own the content rhythm for the venue without being micromanaged

You proactively request the footage and assets you need from the venue team

You can turn raw footage into multiple pieces of polished content quickly

You understand the weekly cycle: tease ? hype ? event ? recap ? repeat

You consistently meet tight deadlines

You produce high-quality work without excessive revisions

You create content that gets people to stop scrolling and buy tickets

You've created content before that has achieved viral or outsized reach





Tools You'll Use



CapCut, Premiere Pro, Final Cut, or similar (video editing)

Adobe Photoshop / Illustrator or Canva (graphics)

Google Drive (file management and asset sharing)

ClickUp (task management)





Working Hours & Compensation



Full-time role (38 hours per week)

Must work during Australian business hours — AWST, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM (full overlap required)

Must be responsive and available during agreed working hours

Salary: $1,200 AUD per month, paid weekly via Wise

Long-term position for the right person, with room to grow



This role is not suitable for freelancers juggling multiple clients. We need someone committed, available, and fast.



How to Apply (Important – Read Carefully)

To apply, you must include:



A short introduction about yourself

Links to nightlife, event, comedy, or entertainment content you have created (not just edited)

At least one example of content you've created that went viral or achieved significant reach — include the metrics (views, shares, engagement)

At least one example of a paid ad creative you produced

At least one example of organic social content

An example of how you've proactively directed or briefed content capture (e.g. shot lists, content requests, footage briefs you've sent to a client or team)

The tools you used to create those examples

Your internet speed and equipment setup



Applications without relevant nightlife, event, or entertainment samples will not be considered.

To confirm you've read this job description, include the word "FREQUENCY" in the first line of your application.



Final Note

Do not send a generic portfolio and hope we find the relevant work. Curate your best nightlife, events, comedy, and entertainment pieces and put them front and centre.

If we have to dig through your portfolio to figure out whether you've done this kind of work before — you're already out.

title: Cold Calling Setter – MedSpa Industry (Base + Commission)
description:
We're a growing agency that helps medspas and aesthetic clinics across the US and Canada reactivate lost patients and grow their revenue. We're looking for a cold calling setter to join our team.



**The Role:



You'll be calling medspa owners and decision-makers to book qualified appointments for our closer. This is a setting position. Your job is to generate interest, handle initial objections, and get the prospect on a call with our team. You are not expected to close deals.

That said, if you perform well and want to grow, there is a clear path to move into a closing role.



**Compensation:



$250/month base salary plus $25 for every appointment that shows. The commission is where the real money is. A strong setter doing 20 to 30 showed appointments per month is earning $750 to $1,000/month total. There is no cap. Top performers also earn additional bonuses based on volume and consistency.



**Trial Period:



This role starts with a 30 day trial period where both sides can walk away if it's not the right fit. If you're producing results, this becomes a long term position with serious earning potential.



**Requirements:



Neutral, clear English with no accent. This is non-negotiable. You'll be speaking directly with business owners in the US and Canada, and you need to sound natural and confident on the phone.

Must be available to make calls during Eastern Time (ET) business hours.

Comfortable with cold calling and handling rejection.

Reliable internet and a quiet calling environment.

Experience in appointment setting, telemarketing, or outbound sales is a strong plus.



Preferred but not required: Female applicants are preferred given that our clients operate in the beauty and aesthetics space, but all qualified applicants are welcome to apply.



**What We Offer:



$250/month base plus $25 per showed appointment with no cap

Performance bonuses on top of commission

A path to a closer role for strong performers

A lean, no-BS team that rewards results

Fully remote. Work from anywhere as long as you can cover ET hours.



** To Apply:



Send a short voice recording (1 to 2 minutes) introducing yourself so we can hear how you sound on the phone. Tell us about any relevant experience and why you'd be a good fit.

title: Virtual Personal Assistant (Phillippines or Thailand) = Personal Admin + Bangkok Local Sourcing
description:
About the Role:

 American real estate investor and global traveler based in Bangkok is looking for a reliable, proactive Virtual Personal Assistant.



This is a fully remote, computer-based role focused on clearing everyday personal admin and “mental load” tasks so the principal can stay focused on high-value work. Most tasks are personal/lifestyle (not core business execution). You will work independently, use AI tools effectively, and occasionally source & coordinate local Bangkok service providers.

Hours: 20–30 hours per week (flexible, results-focused) Location: 100% remote (strong preference for Philippines or Thailand-based candidates for time-zone overlap with Bangkok UTC+7)

Key Responsibilities

• Handle recurring personal admin: cancel subscriptions/autopays, license/renewal tracking, bill follow-ups, research, online shopping/returns, and other items that tend to stay on the principal’s mind.

• Manage and update family + executive calendar; send concise proactive weekly summaries.

• Coordinate personal travel, bookings,
s, and general life admin.

• Source, screen, quote, and coordinate Bangkok-based service providers (handymen, plumbers, vendors, temporary help, etc.) for one-off household needs.

• Participate in short daily or 3x-weekly check-in calls (10–15 minutes).

• Collaborate with the principal using AI tools (primarily Grok and Claude) while applying strong human judgment.

Requirements

• Excellent written and spoken English (fluent).

• Proven experience as a personal or executive virtual assistant.

• Comfortable and effective using AI tools daily.

• Strong initiative, organization, and follow-through.

• Thai language or local Bangkok knowledge is a big plus (but not required).

• Reliable internet and quiet workspace.

What We Offer

• Virtual (Philippines/Remote): $7–$9 per hour (20–30 hrs/week) – paid weekly via Wise/Payoneer.

• Bangkok-based option: ?30,000–40,000 per month (~20-30 hrs/week, results-focused).

• Clear path to more hours, higher rate, and additional responsibilities as you prove reliability and initiative.

• Long-term collaboration with a straightforward, respectful principal.

How to Apply Only applications with a 30–60 second video will be reviewed. In English, please record and attach a short video covering:

1. Why you are a great fit for this role.

2. Your relevant experience.

3. How you believe you can best help.

Please start your application message with the word “READY” so I know you read the full post.

title: Operations Virtual Assistant (Part-Time) – Dropshipping / European Market
description:
We are looking for an experienced Operations Virtual Assistant with hands-on experience in dropshipping order management to join our growing eCommerce team.

This is not a customer support role. This position is fully focused on operational order management, supplier coordination, and internal task management.

The ideal candidate has direct experience working with Service Point (SP) as a supplier platform and understands the daily workflows of a dropshipping operation in the European market.



Responsibilities



Manage daily order operations via Service Point dashboard

Review and approve supplier quotations before the 7:00 AM CET deadline

mbers

title: Creative Strategist and Production Coordinator (E-commerce)
description:
Creative Strategist & Production Coordinator (E-commerce)



We’re hiring a Creative Strategist & Production Coordinator to help us scale our paid creative output for Meta.



This role sits between creative strategy and execution. You will research winning ad concepts, identify ideas worth testing for our brand, adapt and write scripts, prepare voiceover and soundtrack drafts, create clear briefs for editors, and coordinate creator content production.



We’re looking for someone with strong creative taste, Meta ad understanding, good written English, and solid execution skills.



What You’ll Own



1. Creative Research & Strategy Support



You will research competitor and adjacent-brand ads, identify concepts and scripts that could work for our brand, and help build a clear pipeline of ideas worth testing. You should be able to recognize strong hooks, understand what makes an ad compelling, and think in terms of “same but different” rather than just copying what already exists.



2. Script Development



You will adapt winning concepts to our brand, write new scripts when needed, and create multiple hook variations for each concept. You should understand how to write for paid social, especially Meta, and know the difference between writing something that sounds good and writing something that is designed to convert.



3. Creative Assembly & Briefing



You will generate voiceovers, pair them with music, and assemble rough audio drafts that can be used for production. You will also turn concepts into clear, structured Notion briefs for editors, including line-by-line shot guidance, references, and relevant asset links. You should have enough editing sense to understand rhythm, pacing, and how a creative should come together.



4. Creator Coordination



You will help source creators for content batches following our SOPs, support outreach and follow-up, keep communication organized, and make sure deliverables are collected and stored properly. You should be comfortable coordinating moving parts, tracking progress, and helping maintain a reliable flow of fresh creator content.



Expected Deliverables :



The role is designed to support a consistent paid creative testing pipeline by following SOPs. The expected output includes:



- Consistent production of new video briefs (8 per month to start)

- Script development, voiceover generation, ongoing research

- 1 creator batch every 1–2 months, typically around 5 creators

- Creative Strategy research 



What We’re Looking For



You are likely a strong fit if you have experience working on Meta creatives for ecommerce brands and understand how paid social ads are structured to perform.



You should have:



- Experience with Meta /
 ad creatives

- strong understanding of hooks, angles, and direct-response creative

- good written English

- the ability to identify winning concepts and adapt them to a brand

- basic to strong editing skills

- Experience in following SOPs

- a good sense of rhythm, pacing, and creative flow

- strong organization and attention to detail

- the ability to turn ideas into clean, actionable briefs

- experience in DTC / ecommerce



Nice to Have



experience using DaVinci Resolve

experience with 11Labs or other voiceover tools

experience working with UGC creators / influencer coordination

experience building briefs in Notion



Payment



This role will start part-time (4 days a week, Monday to Thursday) moving toward full-time.

Compensation will depend on experience and proof of past results, but our initial target range is around $11.7/hour - $1500/month to start, with room to grow for the right person as results and responsibilities expand.



How to Apply



Please include:

a short note on your experience with Meta ad creatives

examples of ecommerce creative work you’ve been involved in

any relevant scriptwriting, creative strategy, editing, or briefing experience

tools you’ve used (for example: DaVinci, 11Labs, Notion, etc.) 



BrandJet I Propelling Brands into Space 

brandjet.eu

title: Email Marketing Assistant **IMMEDIATE OPENING**
description:
Email Marketing Assistant



Date With the Muse



[
](
)



If you can commit to and live with the following principles, then you are the type of person who will be successful and help our company thrive. If you feel this level of engagement is not right for you or that you’re not willing or able to participate with us at this level, we are not a good fit for you.



Our expectation is that you will take the steps necessary to do what you say you are going to do and be accountable for your actions. You are committed to excellence and take pride in a job well done.



We understand that not every person is ready for this level of performance and we appreciate the honesty of those who d
 this is not the right place for them. On the other hand, you would make an ideal candidate to join our company if you are willing to commit to the following principles:



- Accountability: see it, own it, solve it, do it

- Become part of the solution

- Respect for others and their feelings

- Act now.

- Ask the question: “What else can I do?”

- Ask the questions: “What coaching do you have for me?” and “What can I do better?”

- Personal ownership and pride

- Reject average

- Show others that you care



Part-Time | Remote

We are seeking a highly detail-oriented, systems-driven
 Marketing Specialist who is deeply experienced in Mailchimp and can own the execution, tracking, and optimization of our
 campaigns from start to finish.

This is not a beginner role. You must already know how to use Mailchimp at an advanced level and be able to execute without hand-holding.



Role Overview

You will be responsible for building, scheduling, tagging, automating, and tracking
 campaigns inside Mailchimp to support launches, lead nurturing, and ongoing marketing initiatives.

Success in this role means:

Campaigns go out accurately and on time

Audience segmentation is intentional and strategic

Automations run cleanly and reliably

Data is tracked and reported in a way that informs decisions



Core Responsibilities

Campaign Execution

Build and schedule
 campaigns in Mailchimp

Format
s for clarity, readability, and brand consistency

Ensure all links, tags, and tracking are correctly implemented

Conduct final QA before sending (no broken links, formatting errors, or tag issues)

Audience Segmentation & Tagging

Apply and manage tags based on lead source, behavior, and engagement

Maintain clean audience organization (no duplication, no tag chaos)

Segment lists for targeted campaigns (launches, webinars, nurture sequences)

Automations

Build and maintain automated
 sequences (welcome flows, nurture sequences, event follow-ups)

Ensure automations trigger correctly based on tags and user behavior

Troubleshoot and fix any automation issues proactively

Data Tracking & Reporting

Track key metrics: open rates, click-through rates, conversions, unsubscribe rates

Provide clear weekly reports with insights (not just numbers)

Identify what’s working, what’s not, and where to optimize

Systems & Maintenance

Maintain a clean, organized Mailchimp backend

Document processes and ensure repeatable workflows

Flag issues before they become problems



Required Experience

Advanced, hands-on experience with Mailchimp (non-negotiable)

Proven ability to:

Build campaigns from scratch

Set up and manage automations

Use tags and segmentation strategically

Track and interpret campaign performance data

Strong attention to detail (you catch what others miss)

Ability to follow SOPs while also thinking critically when something doesn’t make sense



Ideal Candidate

Systems thinker (you see how all the pieces connect)

Proactive (you don’t wait to be told something is broken)

Organized and methodical

Comfortable working independently and owning outcomes

Able to execute quickly without sacrificing precision



Definition of Done

A task is complete when:

The campaign is sent or automation is live

All tags and segmentation are correctly applied

All links and tracking are verified

Performance is tracked and reported



Commitment & Reliability (Non-Negotiable)

This role requires absolute reliability and follow-through.

Campaigns will often be scheduled days or weeks in advance

You are expected to complete work ahead of deadlines—not at the last minute

You must ensure you have working access to Mailchimp at all times (login issues are not an acceptable blocker)

If something is not scheduled when it should be, that is a failure of the role

If an issue arises, you are expected to flag it early—not when a campaign is about to go out

This is not a “log in when you feel like it” position.

 This is a time-sensitive execution role tied directly to revenue.

## To Apply



Qualified applicants are encouraged to apply as soon as possible. To express interest please submit 



a cover letter addressed to the CEO detailing why you are uniquely qualified for this role,



a resume,



and a DISC profile 



 materials to 
 with the headline



Interviews will be conducted on a rolling basis. This position begins ASAP for the right candidate. 



Applications received through this platform/website will not move forward.

title: CAD/Renderer Specialist
description:
APPLY HERE: 
/apply/



Blueprint Workforce is a U.S.-based workforce partner for construction, design, and trade businesses. We don't place generic virtual assistants. We place specialists into defined roles with structured onboarding, clear SOPs, and ongoing support.

We're hiring a CAD/Renderer Specialist to produce construction drawings, 3D models, and photorealistic renderings for U.S.-based architecture, design, and construction firms.



This is a technical production role. You'll draft, model, render, and revise. You'll turn sketches and redlines into buildable drawings and client-ready visuals. Precision and speed both matter.

WHAT YOU'LL DO:



-Draft and produce construction documents, floor plans, elevations, sections, and detail drawings

-Create 3D models from architectural plans, sketches, or reference images

-Produce photorealistic renderings for client presentations, marketing, and design approvals

-Revise drawings based on redlines, markups, and design changes

-Maintain drawing standards, layer management, and file organization per firm protocols

-Coordinate with architects, designers, and project managers on drawing sets and deadlines

-Prepare drawing packages for permitting, bidding, and construction

-Perform quality checks on drawings for accuracy, dimensions, and code compliance



TOOLS YOU SHOULD KNOW (some or all):



-AutoCAD

-Revit

-SketchUp

-Rhino

-V-Ray, Lumion, or Enscape (rendering)

-Bluebeam Revu



WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR:



-2+ years of experience in CAD drafting, 3D modeling, or architectural rendering

-Strong proficiency in at least two of the tools listed above

-Portfolio or work samples demonstrating construction drawings and/or renderings (required)

-Understanding of construction documentation standards and building systems

-Architecture, engineering, or interior design educational background preferred

You deliver clean, accurate work and manage revisions without losing detail

You can manage multiple drawing sets and deadlines simultaneously

-Strong written English communication for coordinating with U.S.-based teams

-You're comfortable working U.S. business hours (aligned with your client's time zone)

-Stable internet connection, professional remote work setup, and a machine capable of running CAD/rendering software



WHAT BLUEPRINT PROVIDES:



-Full-time, long-term placement (this is not project-based or seasonal)

-Structured 48-hour onboarding with role-specific training and SOPs

-Dedicated support specialist for your first 90 days

-Regular check-ins: Day 3, Day 30, Day 60, Day 90, then quarterly

-Payroll processed through Gusto (direct deposit)

-10 days PTO annually (after 90-day probation) + 5 sick days (available immediately)

-A company that invests in your success, not just your output



WHY BLUEPRINT IS DIFFERENT:



Most offshore staffing companies hand you a job title and disappear. Blueprint doesn't.

Every placement goes through an 81-question behavioral assessment matched across 6 dimensions. You're not just "available"; you're matched to a role where you'll actually perform and grow.



You get structured onboarding, real support, and a team that treats you as a professional, not a commodity.

We retain 98% of our placements at 6 months. That's not an a
. It's because we build systems that set you up to succeed.



HOW TO APPLY:



APPLY HERE: 
/apply/



Complete your application through our platform. The process includes a behavioral assessment that matches you to the right role and the right client. No generic applications. No guessing.



If you're ready to be placed, not just hired, apply now.


/apply/

title: Executive Operations Assistant – Real Estate
description:
About KRMP Assets

KRMP Assets is a commercial real estate acquisition team focused on sourcing and securing off market multifamily opportunities across key U.S. markets. We work directly with property owners, brokers, and investors to identify deals, structure opportunities, and connect serious buyers to real transactions.



Role: Executive Operations Assistant – Real Estate



You will operate at the center of deal flow, supporting both outreach and backend operations. This is not a basic EA role. You will be directly involved in live transactions, investor communications, and deal execution.



Responsibilities;

40% – Outreach

• Cold outreach via
, phone, and SMS to broker,  property owners and potential buyers

• Warm follow ups with existing leads, investors, and contacts

• Maintain consistent communication flow across all channels



60% – Operations & Deal Support

• Manage and maintain CRM (GHL), spreadsheets, and internal databases

title: Sales Closer (MedSpa) — High-Ticket Consultations + Upsells (U.S. Clients)
description:
About the Role



We’re a fast-growing MedSpa looking for a rockstar sales closer who can convert warm leads into booked appointments and confidently upsell premium treatment plans. You’ll speak with clients across the United States—by phone, SMS, and
—guiding them from curiosity to commitment with a high-trust, consultative approach.



This is not “order taking.” This is ethical persuasion, a clear recommendation, and a strong closing—while delivering a luxury client experience.



What You’ll Do (Key Responsibilities)

1. Close warm inbound leads into consultations, treatment packages, and memberships (phone-first).

2. Run consultative sales calls: ask great questions, uncover goals/pain points, recommend the right plan.

3. Upsell and cross-sell (e.g., packages, add-ons, skincare, post-care, membership upgrades) in a natural, helpful way.

4. Handle objections with confidence: pricing, timing, spouse approval, fear/anxiety, and “I need to think about it.”

5. Drive show rate and reduce cancellations/no-shows with follow-up and confirmation processes.

6. Follow up consistently via SMS +
 + phone, using clear sequences and strong call-to-actions.

7. Maintain clean CRM notes: call outcomes, next steps, client preferences, and treatment interests.

8. Collaborate with providers and the front desk to ensure a seamless handoff and exceptional experience.

9. Meet and exceed weekly/monthly targets and KPIs.



What Success Looks Like (KPIs You’ll Own)

1. Lead-to-booked conversion rate

2. Close rate on consultations/treatment plans

3. Average order value (AOV) & upsell rate

4. Show rate / reduction in no-shows

5. Speed-to-lead and follow-up compliance

6. Revenue generated per week/month



Who You Are

Must-Haves

1. Proven closer with 2+ years in high-converting phone sales (MedSpa, aesthetics, dental, wellness, or high-ticket services strongly preferred).

2. Strong experience selling packages, memberships, and recurring plans (not just single sessions).

3. You’re confident and persuasive without being pushy—clients feel supported, not sold.

4. Excellent verbal communication: warm tone, clear structure, strong control of the call.

5. Objection-handling is your strength. You can close with empathy and certainty.

6. Tech-comfortable: CRM pipelines, call tracking, texts,
 sequences, and reporting.

7. Reliable, responsive, and consistent—your follow-up game is elite.



Nice-to-Haves

1. Experience with MedSpa platforms/CRMs (e.g., GoHighLevel, Boulevard, PatientPop, HubSpot).

2. Understanding of common aesthetic services (injectables, lasers, facials, body contouring, and skincare plans).

3. Background in performance-based sales environments with commission structures.



Schedule & Work Style

1. Work hours aligned to U.S. time zones (typically Eastern/Central coverage).

2. Availability for peak lead times: afternoons/evenings and some weekends (depending on demand).

3. Fast-paced environment with clear targets, coaching, and room to grow.



Tools You’ll Use

1. CRM + pipeline management

2. Calling + texting system

3. Booking/scheduling platform

4. Scripts, FAQs, pricing sheets, and offer frameworks (we’ll provide them—your job is to execute and improve)



Compensation

1. Base + commission ($15/appointment) + performance bonuses (top performers earn top-tier income).

2. Clear growth path into Senior Closer / Sales Manager / Revenue Operations as the team scales.



Why You’ll Love It Here

1. Warm leads + strong demand (you’re not cold-calling random lists all day).

2. Premium services people genuinely want—sold the right way.

3. A team that values performance, client experience, and long-term relationships.

title: Acquisitions Closer — Distressed Real Estate (US Market) | $800/mo Base + Deal Bonuses ($1,500+/mo Realistic)
description:
*Who We Are*

Best Offer For Home LLC is a US-based real estate acquisitions company that buys deeply distressed properties in Texas — mostly from families dealing with tax-delinquent homes, deceased owners, and unclear ownership situations. We solve problems other investors walk away from, then resell the properties.

We are NOT a call center. We are a small, high-performance team. You will work directly with the owner.





*The Role*

You will be our primary acquisitions closer — calling property owners and heirs in Texas, building rapport, negotiating, and locking up deals under contract.

Deal mix you’ll be working:

 • 60–70% wholesale deals — properties we put under contract and assign to another buyer. Most have solvable title issues (missing heir signatures, old liens, probate gaps) that we resolve at the contract level.

 • 30–40% direct deed purchases with curative work — we buy the deed, fix the title issues ourselves, and resell on MLS.

This means every call involves some complexity. You are not reading a script to a motivated seller. You are talking to:

 • Heirs of deceased property owners

 • Elderly homeowners behind on taxes

 • Out-of-state family members who inherited a headache

 • People who are grieving, confused, or suspicious

This is emotional, consultative selling. Not pressure sales.





*What You’ll Do*

 • Make 60–100 outbound calls per day to tax-delinquent property owners and heirs

 • Qualify leads against our buying criteria (we’ll teach you)

 • Build rapport, uncover motivation, and present offers

 • Lock deals under contract using our standard purchase agreement

 • Update our CRM (Close) after every call with detailed notes

 • Coordinate with our curative team to move files toward closing

 • Follow up relentlessly on warm leads — most deals close after 3–8 touches





* What We Need From You*

Non-negotiables:

 • 3+ years of US-based phone sales experience — real estate, insurance, debt collection, mortgage, or probate-related preferred

 • Neutral or near-neutral English accent — you will be on the phone with Texans every day

 • Emotional intelligence — you can talk to a 72-year-old widow about her late husband’s house without sounding like a telemarketer

 • Resilience — you will be hung up on, cussed at, and ghosted. You keep dialing.

 • Strong written English — you’ll be writing CRM notes and short
s

 • Reliable internet, quiet workspace, USB headset, backup power

 • Available 9 AM – 6 PM Central Time (US) — this is non-negotiable

You will thrive here if:

 • You’ve closed deals, not just set appointments

 • You’re comfortable with messy, ambiguous situations

 • You take ownership instead of waiting for instructions

 • You want to grow with a company, not job-hop

Do not apply if:

 • You’ve only done appointment setting or lead qualification

 • You need a rigid script to function

 • You’re uncomfortable discussing death, family conflict, or money

 • You’re juggling multiple clients





*Compensation*

 • Base: $800/month (paid weekly)

 • Contract bonus: $50 per signed purchase agreement that passes our review

 • Closing bonus: $500 per file that closes

 • Realistic earnings for a strong closer: $1,500+/month — and higher as volume grows

We want you to earn real money here. The base keeps your lights on. The bonuses are where you build a life. Top performers will out-earn the base several times over.





*How to Apply*

Applications without all three items below will be deleted without review.

 1. Record a 3–5 minute Loom video answering:

 • Your name, location, and current work situation

 • Your most difficult sales call ever — what happened, how you handled it, what the outcome was

 • Roleplay: You’re calling the adult son of a deceased homeowner whose property is 3 years tax delinquent. He picks up. Go. (You have 60 seconds.)

 2. Written response (in the application body, not an attachment):

 • Why you want THIS role specifically (not “I need a job”)

 • Your fastest internet speed test result (screenshot link)

 • Your expected monthly base salary

 3. Subject line must read: Closer Application — [Your First Name] — Texas

Applications missing the subject line format will not be reviewed. This is your first test.





*Timeline*

 • Applications reviewed on a rolling basis

 • Finalists will complete a paid 1-hour mock-call test

 • Start date: ASAP for the right person

We are building something real. If that excites you, show us.

description:

Looking to build a car dealership website. We have a few dealerships and partners so we will need a good number of such websites. Looking to start on task/gig basis and move part time ASAP.



- Wordpress/Elementor professioanls only! (please do not apply if you don't have amazing wordpress/elementor design skills)

- must be able to configure & setup a dealership theme fast and accurate

- must have specific live examples of dealership you built yourself recently (we need samples, verification, we want to make sure you know dealerships so we don't waste each other's time)



Must be independently and be able to build the entire theme/site when given a business name, branding, logo and the installed theme. You make it ready for our dealerships, end to end (2-3 cars should be uploaded as an example to start the inventory).



Looking for SOLID BUILD & reasonable build time - ONLY someone with experience in dealership themes and dealership building please.



Thank you!!

XDigital24

title: Ecommerce Operations Manager
description:
Ikigai Cases is hiring for an Ecommerce Operations Manager

Location: Remote

Compensation: $1800 per month + performance-based bonuses



About the Company

Ikigai Cases (
) is a family-owned e-commerce business selling premiu
tal pill cases. Founded in December 2021, the business has been operated by two brothers from Washington, DC since then. After four years, the company has grown to selling thousands of pill cases every month and double-digit tea
mbers, including a thriving remote team.



Our mission is simple: to make exceptional pill cases and provide exceptional customer service. We uphold this commitment by managing our fulfillment operations in-house and offering white-glove service to our customers.



We are looking to hire an Ecommerce Operations Manager to join our growing remote team.



5 Core Responsibilities

1. Launch Execution: Plan and execute all sales, product launches, color launches, and promotional 
nts, ensuring offers, timing, assets, and systems go live correctly.



2. Cross-Functional Launch Coordination: Coordinate all teams involved in launches (Marketing,
, Creative, Website, Ops, CX, Fulfillment), managing timelines and dependencies to ensure aligned execution.



3. Website & Systems Setup for Launches: Set up and configure all website, Shopify, and related systems required for launches, including products, collections, discounts, carts, and launch-specific site changes.



4. Launch QA & System Hygiene: Own pre-launch QA and post-launch cleanup, ensuring accuracy across offers and flows and maintaining clean, current systems with no leftover artifacts.



5. Launch Process Improvement: Improve launch workflows, templates, and safeguards to support increasing launch volume and complexity with reduced risk.



Why You Should Take This Role

-You’ll be working at a brand that is obsessed with their customers and has built a loyal following. Just read a few of their reviews: 

-You’ll be working at a company that has scaled up extremely quickly - while focusing on the operational side of the business. Now that the operations are stabilized with key roles in place, we are focused on investing in growth.

-You’ll be working with people who have extreme levels of competitive fire in their bellies - people who will run through walls to make sure the company succeeds.

-You’ll be working hand-in-hand with the founders of the company - building the systems and growth strategy together.

-You’ll be working at a company with a robust product development roadmap that includes new colors / products releasing monthly



Required Experience & Skills Experience

-3+ years working directly on an e-commerce brand, with hands-on responsibility for executing sales, product launches, or promotional 
nts (not just website maintenance).

-Proven experience owning launches end-to-end, including setup, coordination, QA, and post-launch cleanup across teams and systems.

-Strong project management experience in e-commerce, coordinating multiple stakeholders to deliver time-sensitive launches.

-Working proficiency with Shopify admin for launch setup (products, collections, discounts, carts, and site changes; no coding required).

-Clear written communication and high attention to detail in revenue-critical work.

-Experience working with Notion or similar project management tools

-Must be based outside the United States.



Preferred Experiences (Nice To Have’s)

-Experience running A/B tests using platforms such as Intelligems.

-Experience using page builders (Replo preferred) in addition to Shopify Theme Editor



Communication & English Proficiency 

-Strong written and verbal English skills.

-Ability to communicate effectively with internal teams and external partners via
.

-Ability to receive and provide constructive feedback.



Compensation Details

-Compensation: $1800 per month + performance-based bonuses

-Work Hours: 50 hours per week, Monday to Friday Business Hours, EST

-Paid Holidays: 10 paid holidays per year

-PTO: 1 week earned for every 6 months worked at the company



To Apply

Fill out the application form here: 



This application includes a number of questions in order to ensure we hire an outstanding candidate for this position, so please be advised in advance that it will take time to complete.



Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Qualified candidates will be contacted for interviews. Applications on OnlineJobs.ph, DMs, and messages sent via
 will not be reviewed.

title: Freelance Web Designer (Figma)
description:
About Us

Pixel Eye Studio is a U.S.-based design agency that builds beautiful, thoughtful websites for mission-driven organizations — nonprofits, religious orders, educational institutions, and purpose-led brands. We care deeply about craft, typography, and creating design that feels intentional rather than templated.



We're looking to add another talented web designer to our roster of freelance collaborators for ongoing project work.



What You'll Do:

• Design modern, custom website layouts in Figma (desktop, tablet, and mobile)

• Translate client brand guidelines into polished, on-brand web design

• Collaborate with our project manager and dev team on handoff and revisions

• Iterate based on feedback from clients and our creative director

• Occasionally refresh or redesign sections of existing client websites



What We're Looking For

• 3+ years of web design experience with a strong portfolio of live websites

• Expert-level Figma skills (components, auto-layout, variants, responsive frames)

• Strong understanding of visual hierarchy, typography, spacing, and color

• Familiarity with designing for WordPress builds (knowing what's realistic to develop)

• Excellent written English and clear communication

• Reliable response times during overlap with U.S. Eastern Time

• Attention to detail and ability to follow brand systems precisely



Nice to Have

• Experience designing for nonprofits, faith-based orgs, or educational institutions

• Basic understanding of SEO principles as they relate to page structure

• Illustration or icon design skills



How to Apply

To be considered, your application must include:

• A link to your portfolio (applications without a portfolio link will not be reviewed)

• A brief note about which 1–2 projects in your portfolio you're most proud of, and why

• Your current availability (hours per week) and preferred hourly rate within our range

• The word "Mandalay" somewhere in your message so we know you read this post carefully



We review every qualified application and reply within 5–7 business days. Looking forward to seeing your work!

title: Remote Intake Coordinator
description:
About the Position: Remote Intake Coordinator - Registered Dietitian Clinic



Health Loft is seeking an Intake Coordinator to join our team. We are looking for an individual with strong communication skills that is motivated, and self-driven. This position would serve as the initial point of contact for new patients, collecting relevant history and insurance information over the phone or via
. This individual would also assist with directing patients to the appropriate department, rescheduling of current clients, scanning and uploading of paper documents/checks, and answering basic billing questions.



About Health Loft:

Health Loft, based in Chicago, Illinois, provides medical nutritional counseling from expert registered dietitians (RD's) to individuals in multiple states. We make it easy for patients to see an RD by accepting their health insurance and offering convenient access to a licensed professional via our telehealth platform.



Our Mission: We understand the life changing work that RD's provide, as well as the difficulty patients have using their health insurance when seeking their care. Our mission is to bridge that gap by strategically growing, leading, and delivering on innovative medical nutrition therapy nationwide. By investing in our team and our patients, we will elevate the individual experience for all clients.

Our Patients: Our focus is on helping as many patients gain access to the knowledge and care of an RD as possible. We see a range of ages and conditions including but not limited to eating disorders, intuitive eating, diabetes, PCOS, weight management, cardiovascular disease, women's health, and more.

Why Join Health Loft:



Our Team: The entire team at Health Loft is focused on making this the best place to work.

Great culture: We strongly believe in working and learning together as a team. Our tea
ets regularly on a weekly bases to discuss successes, areas for learning and development, and goals. We also make a point to schedule team-building and social events when possible (Covid dependent).

We listen: We believe our tea
mbers are our greatest source of innovation and we actively work to incorporate feedback to make Health Loft a better place for our tea
mbers and clients alike.

Flexibility: Enjoy the ability to work from home, skipping the lengthy commute and expense of having to go into the office.

Our Core Values



Excellence

Growth Mindset

Patient Centricity

Autonomy

Job responsibilities includes but not limited to:



Answer all incoming calls promptly, politely, and take/relay messages as necessary.

Following up with via phone and
 to schedule new patient leads.

Schedule new and existing patients in accordance with clinic policy.

Maintain all patient charts according to company policy.

Verify eligibility and benefits

Answer basic billing questions

Troubleshoot client issues

Maintain patient confidentiality.

Responsible for tracking and documentation of referrals and new patient leads.

Complete assigned projects on time and within parameters as directed by management.

Collect, scan, and upload paper claim documents and checks.

Other duties as assigned by management.

Qualifications:



Must have worked in the medical field or related field within the last two years



High School diploma or GED required. Associate degree preferred.

Excellent communication skills and organizational skills.

Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

Ability to multi-task.

Experience with Google Workspace a +

Previous experience using EMR/EHR system.

Must demonstrate effective communication skills.

Basic medical terminology preferred

title: Real Estate VA (Listing Coordinator)
description:
Are you the detail-oriented, deadline-driven Real Estate Listing Coordinator we’ve been looking for?

We’re hiring a Remote Real Estate Listing Coordinator to support our U.S.-based real estate business. This is a work-from-home position where you’ll manage listings from onboarding to active status, ensure accuracy across platforms, and keep all listing-related tasks on track.



If you’re highly organized, thrive in a fast-paced environment, and enjoy keeping listings compliant and up to date, this role is for you.



Key Responsibilities:

- Coordinate new listings from onboarding through activation and updates.

- Enter, manage, and update listings in MLS and other listing platforms.

- Collect, organize, and maintain listing documents, disclosures, and property details.

- Coordinate with agents, sellers, photographers, and vendors.

- Track listing deadlines and ensure compliance with MLS and brokerage requirements.

- Make price changes, status updates, and listing revisions as needed.

- Maintain accurate records and communicate listing updates to the team.



What We’re Looking For:

- Prior experience as a Real Estate Listing Coordinator that worked for A US Real Estate company is a MUST.

- Familiarity with MLS systems and real estate listing workflows.

- Fluent in spoken & written English (must keep up with natural conversation).

- Strong attention to detail and organizational skills.

- Tech-savvy and comfortable working across multiple tools and platforms.

- Self-motivated and able to work independently with minimal supervision.



What You’ll Gain:

- A long-term, remote role with stable workload.

- Opportunity to work closely with a U.S.-based real estate team.

- Hands-on experience managing real estate listings end-to-end.

- Meaningful work where your accuracy and organization directly impact success.

title: GRAPHIC DESIGNER (CANVA + PHOTOSHOP) — MENU CREATION + FAST TURNAROUND ONLY
description:
GRAPHIC DESIGNER (CANVA + PHOTOSHOP) — MENU CREATION + FAST TURNAROUND ONLY



We are a busy café/restaurant in the U.S. looking for a high-level Graphic Designer who can create professional menu designs, update digital menus, handle various graphic tasks, and do occasional basic tech support (such as uploading graphics to Yodeck or Google Business).



This is a serious long-term position.

If you are not experienced or not reliable, do NOT apply.



?



???? MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES

 • Create menu designs, menu updates, and menu boards

 • Insert product photos into menu templates

 • Create clean, modern promotional graphics for the business

 • Canva + Photoshop work (image cleanup, cropping, improving photos, layout adjustments)

 • Update graphics for Google Business Profile / Google Maps

 • Upload designs to Yodeck for our digital screens (easy — we will show you)

 • Maintain brand consistency across all designs



There is NO social media content creation required.



?



???? REQUIREMENTS

 • Strong Canva skills (non-negotiable)

 • Real Photoshop experience

 • A very good eye for design — spacing, clean layout, color harmony

 • Excellent English and communication

 • Ability to produce accurate work on short deadlines

 • Must be able to complete tasks in under 24 hours (most jobs are same-day or next-day)

 • Must be reliable, honest, and focused

 • Must NOT outsource the work to someone else — if you do, you will be fired immediately



?



?? STRICT STANDARDS



Please read this carefully:

 • If you lie about your experience, you will be fired.

 • If you waste time, delay deadlines, or produce low-quality work, you will be fired.

 • If you try to outsource the work to another person, you will be fired instantly.

 • If you cannot communicate clearly or disappear during tasks, you will be removed.



We do not want to waste time. We need someone serious, consistent, and committed.



If you take on multiple jobs at once and cannot meet deadlines, this is not for you.



?



???? COMPENSATION

 • Competitive hourly rate based on skill and experience

 • Raises available for excellent, consistent work

 • Long-term stable position for the right person



?



???? HOW TO APPLY



Apply directly through OnlineJobs.ph with:

 1. Your portfolio (menu designs or business graphics preferred)

 2. Your Canva + Photoshop samples

 3. A short note about your experience

 4. Your expected hourly rate



Only apply if you are truly confident in your skills and can deliver fast, clean, high-quality work.

title: Account Manager
description:
About Us 



LecsIT is a growing managed IT services company that helps small and mid-sized businesses stay secure, productive, and confident in their technology. We focus on long-term relationships, proactive support, and clear communication—not ticket factories or one-size-fits-all IT. 



The Role 



We’re looking for an Account Manager who thrives at the intersection of relationships and technology. This role is all about owning client relationships, translating business needs into IT solutions, and making sure customers feel heard, supported, and strategically guided. 



You’ll act as the primary point of contact for assigned clients, working closely with our technical team to ensure service delivery aligns with expectations while also identifying opportunities to improve the client’s IT posture. 



Key Responsibilities 

-Serve as the primary relationship owner for a portfolio of clients 

-Conduct regular check-ins, quarterly business reviews, and strategic planning conversations 

-Translate technical concepts into clear, business-friendly language 

-Identify upsell and cross-sell opportunities aligned with client goals (security, compliance, cloud, etc.) 

-Coordinate with internal technical teams to ensure smooth delivery and follow-through 

-Advocate for the client internally while protecting company standards and scope 

-Track client satisfaction and proactively address risks before they become issues 



What Success Looks Like 

-Clients feel confident, informed, and cared for 

-Renewals are smooth and predictable 

-Expansion opportunities are uncovered naturally through trust 

-Clear documentation of client needs, decisions, and next steps 



Qualifications 

-2+ years in account management, customer success, or client-facing roles 

-Experience in IT, MSP, SaaS, or technology services strongly preferred 

-Strong communication and relationship-building skills 

-Comfortable discussing security, compliance, and IT strategy at a high level 

-Organized, proactive, and able to manage multiple accounts effectively 



Bonus Points 

-MSP or IT services experience 

-Familiarity with Microsoft 365, cybersecurity concepts, or compliance frameworks 

-Experience conducting QBRs or executive-level conversations 



What We Offer 

-Competitive salary with performance-based incentives 

-Flexible work environment 

-Opportunity to grow with a fast-moving, relationship-driven IT company 

-Direct impact on client success and company growth



To apply, please provide a short voice introduction via 
.

title: Prepress / Artwork QA Specialist
description:
About Us



We are a UK-based signage and vehicle graphics company producing high volumes of artwork for print. Accuracy is critical — mistakes lead to costly reprints, so we are building a dedicated QA process to ensure every file is 100% correct before production.



Role Overview



We are looking for a detail-obsessed QA specialist to check artwork before it goes to print.



This is NOT a creative design role. Your responsibility is to carefully inspect files and ensure they are accurate, correctly scaled, and match the customer brief exactly.



You will be the final checkpoint before production.



Key Responsibilities

Check all artwork against the original job brief before print

Verify measurements and scaling (especially vehicle wheelbase and layout)

Ensure correct use of templates and positioning

Check all text:

spelling

phone numbers

 addresses

URLs

Confirm correct icons, branding, and layout rules are followed

Open and review files in Adobe Illustrator and/or Adobe Acrobat

Ensure files are print-ready (no missing elements, correct setup)

Flag ANY issues clearly before approval

Complete a QA checklist for every job

Important



You must be comfortable saying:



“This job is NOT ready for print”



Accuracy is more important than speed.



Requirements

Strong attention to detail (this is the most important requirement)

Experience in:

print production, signage, OR prepress checking

Ability to read and compare job briefs carefully

Good written English

Comfortable working with measurements and layouts

Experience using Adobe Illustrator and/or Adobe Acrobat

Reliable and consistent

Nice to Have (Not Required)

Experience with vehicle graphics or large format print

Understanding of scale drawings or templates

How to Apply



To apply, you must complete a short test:



We will provide sample artwork files with intentional mistakes.

Your task is to identify ALL errors before print.



This is the most important part of the hiring process.



Why Join Us

Long-term, stable work

Clear systems and processes

Opportunity to grow with the business

Performance-based increases

Working Hours



Flexible, but must overlap with UK working hours for communication when needed.



Pay



Based on experience (part-time to start, with opportunity to increase)



Final Note



We are looking for someone who takes pride in catching mistakes others miss.



If you are highly detail-oriented and enjoy checking work for accuracy, we want to hear from you.
